typeData.behavior:
; 7486 — quad 2-input EXCLUSIVE-OR gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is HIGH when its two inputs differ and
; LOW when they are equal.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: xor fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). The XOR output is active-high and
; written as the two differing-input product terms: nY = nA*/nB + /nA*nB.
1Y = 1A * /1B + /1A * 1B
2Y = 2A * /2B + /2A * 2B
3Y = 3A * /3B + /3A * 3B
4Y = 4A * /4B + /4A * 4B

3Y = 3A * /3B + /3A * 3B

typeData.behavior:
; 7408 — quad 2-input AND gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is HIGH only when both of that
; gate's inputs are HIGH, and LOW otherwise.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: and fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). Each output is a single AND term
; at the output's active-high polarity: nY = nA * nB.
1Y = 1A * 1B
2Y = 2A * 2B
3Y = 3A * 3B
4Y = 4A * 4B

; 7408 — quad 2-input AND gate. Derived from the function table

;

1Y = 1A * 1B
2Y = 2A * 2B
3Y = 3A * 3B
4Y = 4A * 4B

; 7432 — quad 2-input OR gate. Derived from the function table (datasheet
; Table 3): each output nY is HIGH when either of that gate's inputs is
; HIGH, and LOW only when both inputs are LOW.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: or fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). The OR output is active-high and
; needs no complement: nY = nA + nB.
1Y = 1A + 1B
2Y = 2A + 2B
3Y = 3A + 3B
4Y = 4A + 4B

4Y = 4A + 4B
